title: Schefflera burchellii
taxonomy: Schefflera burchellii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae. It belongs to the genus Schefflera and is native to South Africa. The classification of Schefflera burchellii is further specified as Schefflera burchellii (Burm.f.) Frodin. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the same family. Schefflera burchellii is also known by the synonyms of Schefflera aralioides and Schefflera burchellii var. burchellii.

The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are often used to treat skin conditions and wounds. intro: Schefflera burchellii, also known as the Yellow Schefflera, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its unique, umbrella-like leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its ability to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Schefflera burchellii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and lignans, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Schefflera burchellii is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in the form of capsules or tablets.
